name: /deep-research workflow
description: Multi-agent research engine that decomposes a topic into sub-questions, dispatches parallel searcher agents, synthesizes findings with citations and confidence levels, runs mandatory challenge phases (contrarian + OTB), verifies claims, pursues gaps, re-synthesizes, and produces structured downstream-consumable output (RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md, claims.jsonl, sources.jsonl, metadata.json) with adapters for deep-plan, skill-creator, and add-debt.
trigger: User slash command /deep-research "<topic>" or spawned by another skill (deep-plan, skill-creator, research-plan-team)
notes: Largest workflow by component count. 12 distinct phases (0–5, 2.5, 3, 3.5, 3.9, 3.95–3.97). Searcher agents receive domain config from domains/<domain>.yaml. Windows 0-byte agent output bug (issue #39791) has documented mitigation in SKILL.md Critical Rule 4. The GSD adapter and research-plan-team are optional integration paths. .research/research-index.jsonl is a cross-session persistent artifact enabling recall/refresh.

name: /deep-plan workflow
description: Discovery-first planning skill that eliminates implementation assumptions through exhaustive Q&A, produces standalone DECISIONS.md + PLAN.md artifacts, runs convergence-loop verification on the plan, and routes to appropriate execution. Uses Explore agent for codebase discovery and convergence-loop for diagnosis verification.
trigger: User slash command /deep-plan "<topic>" or invoked by research-plan-team
notes: Phase 0 checks for prior brainstorm + research. Phase 3.5 runs CL verification on the plan. REFERENCE.md documents /gsd:new-project and /gsd:plan-phase routing alternatives (SoNash-only — routing docs only, not execution deps). skill-creator consumes deep-plan patterns. D-slots reference /repo-analysis output as a potential input source (SoNash-specific). Team can be used for large research-to-plan pipelines.

name: /brainstorm workflow
description: Structured ideation workflow using Socratic dialogue, multi-direction evaluation, and mandatory contrarian checkpoint, producing a BRAINSTORM.md that feeds into /deep-plan or /deep-research. Uses convergence-loop for Phase 4 verification and optionally spawns a contrarian-challenger agent.
notes: The contrarian-challenger agent is conditional (spawned for stress-testing in Phase 3). Extractions files (.research/EXTRACTIONS.md) are project-level artifacts that may not exist in a fresh install — skill handles missing gracefully. BRAINSTORM.md output feeds directly into deep-plan or deep-research. Phase 4 CL-verify can use convergence-loop programmatic mode.

name: /session-begin workflow
description: Session pre-flight checklist that loads SESSION_CONTEXT.md, increments session counter, checks git branch, checks hook anomaly log, runs stale-doc detection, and produces a goal-selection prompt. Fires hooks (check-mcp-servers.js, compact-restore.js) automatically at session start via settings.json wiring.
trigger: User slash command /session-begin OR new Claude Code session start (hooks fire automatically)
notes: Two layers: the automatic hook layer (fires on every SessionStart via settings.json) and the manual skill layer (run by user or skill trigger). The hook layer is always active; the skill layer is optional. compact-restore.js only fires on 'compact' matcher, not ordinary SessionStart. Many phases are DEFERRED in JASON-OS v0.1 pending Layer 2 hooks. SESSION_CONTEXT.md 5-field contract is the key data contract.

name: /session-end workflow
description: Session closure pipeline: updates SESSION_CONTEXT.md (5-field contract), reviews agent compliance state, runs session-end-commit.js to commit changes, optionally pushes. Produces a session learning capture via session-end-learnings.md. Multiple phases gated on Layer 2 hooks in JASON-OS v0.1.
notes: Hard-coded plan target .planning/jason-os-mvp/PLAN.md (D33) will need updating when second plan dir exists. Phase 3 (metrics pipeline) stripped entirely for v0. Phase 2 (compliance) mostly Layer-2-gated. session-end-commit.js does the final git commit + push via execFileSync. session-end-learnings.md is the append-only retrospective. Pre-compaction-save.js hook is a parallel participant that captures state BEFORE compaction separately from this skill.

name: /pr-review workflow
description: PR code review processing pipeline: receives pasted review content from Qodo/SonarCloud, applies DAS scoring, categorizes items, fixes in priority order via targeted agents, tracks deferrals via /add-debt, captures learnings, and produces structured state files per PR+round for cross-session tracking.
trigger: User slash command /pr-review with pasted review content; also triggers GitHub Actions (sonarcloud.yml Qodo GitHub App) on PR creation/update
notes: Two integration surfaces: (1) external bots produce review content that user pastes in; (2) the skill processes that content. The sonarcloud.yml CI workflow and Qodo GitHub App run autonomously on PR events — they are not invoked by the skill, but their output is consumed by it. State files are per-PR+round to survive compaction. SONAR_PROJECT_KEY must be configured per-deployment. Gemini reviewer excluded from JASON-OS v0 port. User paste is required source of truth — gh pr view truncates/loses collapsed sections.

name: /pre-commit-fixer workflow
description: Diagnose and fix pre-commit hook failures via classify → fix → structured-report → user-confirm → re-commit cycle. Works for any failure category emitted by .husky/pre-commit (currently only gitleaks in JASON-OS v0.1). Optionally routes unfixable items to /add-debt.
trigger: Pre-commit hook failure (git commit rejected) OR user slash command /pre-commit-fixer; also referenced by CLAUDE.md guardrail #9
notes: Foundation-scoped to gitleaks-only. Recipe categories for ESLint, Prettier, tsc, lint-staged, pattern-compliance, cognitive-complexity, cross-doc-deps armed but inactive. SKIP_REASON can only be set with explicit user authorization (CLAUDE.md §4.14). CLAUDE.md guardrail #9 mandates using this skill on pre-commit failure. After 2 fix attempts on same category, hard stop + ask user.

name: pre-commit security gate process
description: Automatic security gate that runs on every git commit: gitleaks secrets detection on staged files. Blocks commit if secrets found. Managed by husky + _shared.sh infrastructure with SKIP_CHECKS/SKIP_REASON discipline. Wired via .husky/pre-commit and npm prepare.
trigger: git commit (any commit) — automatic, not user-invoked
notes: Entirely automatic — no user invocation required. Advisory degradation if gitleaks not installed (warns, does not block). 14+ additional checks planned as post-Foundation deferrals (ESLint, tests, lint-staged, pattern-compliance, propagation-staged, audit-s0s1, skill-validation, cognitive-complexity, cross-doc-deps, doc-headers, doc-index, agent-compliance, debt-schema, JSONL-MD sync). Each is a single-line addition to pre-commit.

name: pre-push escalation gate process
description: Automatic pre-push gate running two checks: (1) escalation-gate that blocks push if unacknowledged error-severity hook warnings exist, (2) block direct push to main/master. Managed by .husky/pre-push + inline Node.js. Block-push-to-main.js Claude Code hook runs as a parallel PreToolUse gate before the Bash layer.
trigger: git push (any push) — automatic; also block-push-to-main.js fires on Bash git push * via Claude Code PreToolUse hook
notes: Two enforcement layers in parallel: (1) .husky/pre-push is the git-layer gate (fires even on manual CLI push), (2) block-push-to-main.js is the Claude Code layer gate (fires when Claude uses Bash to push). Together they close the enforcement gap where a single-layer approach could be bypassed. 11+ additional checks planned as deferrals.

name: compaction state preservation process
description: Automatic state snapshot taken before context compaction: pre-compaction-save.js captures task states, commit log, git context, agent summaries, active plan, session notes, and active audits to handoff.json. On session resume after compaction, compact-restore.js injects handoff.json into Claude's context window.
trigger: Context compaction event (PreCompact hook) — automatic; recovery fires on SessionStart with 'compact' matcher
notes: Two-step process: save (PreCompact) + restore (SessionStart/compact). pre-compaction-save.js is the most complex of the pair (16.7KB). Stale handoff (>60 min) is silently skipped by compact-restore.js. This process is what makes skill /checkpoint the manual variant — checkpoint writes handoff.json explicitly for mid-session use.

name: commit tracking and health alert process
description: PostToolUse hook that fires on every Bash call: detects new git commits, appends structured metadata to commit-log.jsonl, logs commit failures, surfaces pre-commit hook output to user, and conditionally spawns mid-session health alerts via mid-session-alerts.js.
trigger: Every Bash tool call (PostToolUse hook) — automatic; internally fast-bails on non-commit bash calls
notes: mid-session-alerts.js is an optional dependency (silently skipped if absent). commit-log.jsonl is Layer A compaction resilience artifact — it persists commit metadata across compaction events. The hook fires on ALL Bash calls but fast-bails internally via regex. CLAUDE.md guardrail #13 mandates reviewing hook summary after every commit.

name: settings and hook integrity protection process
description: PreToolUse gate on Write/Edit operations that blocks modifications to .claude/settings.json or settings.local.json that would remove critical hooks (block-push-to-main.js, settings-guardian.js). Large-file gate blocks reads of files >5MB. Both fire before their respective tool operations.
trigger: Write or Edit tool call on settings.json (settings-guardian.js); Read tool call on any file (large-file-gate.js) — both automatic PreToolUse
notes: settings-guardian.js has continueOnError:false — it is a hard-blocking gate. large-file-gate.js has continueOnError:true — it warns but allows. settings-guardian.js CRITICAL_HOOKS list is hardcoded — must be updated when porting if hook names differ. SKIP_GATES=1 env var bypasses large-file-gate. Both write to hook-warnings-log.jsonl.

name: memory sync and canonical-memory flow
description: Cross-session persistent memory system: canonical-memory files in .claude/canonical-memory/ are the authoritative source; user-home files in ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/ are the active working set loaded each session. Updates to user-home memories flow from session interactions. Canonical memories are synced by explicit user action or session-end.
trigger: Automatic load on every session (user-home MEMORY.md injected into system prompt); explicit update via Claude memory write operations
notes: The canonical-memory/ directory (12 files) is a SUBSET of the user-home memory directory (60+ files). User-home has ~48 additional feedback memories not yet promoted to canonical. This gap is a known schema divergence. Sync direction: user-home is more current; canonical is more curated. No automated sync hook exists — promotion is manual. session-end-learnings.md is the only project-scoped file in canonical-memory. The memory loading mechanism is built into Claude Code (MEMORY.md in system-reminder).

name: /todo workflow
description: Cross-session todo management system with 8-option interactive menu (add, edit, complete, progress, delete, reprioritize, archive, list), JSONL-backed persistence via advisory-locked CLI, and auto-rendered TODOS.md. Protects against data loss through regression guards and mutation-only-via-CLI discipline.
trigger: User slash command /todo with subcommand (add/edit/complete/etc.) or interactive menu
notes: T30 fix mandates all mutations go through todos-cli.js — direct JSONL edits bypass regression guard and cause data loss. todos-mutations.js is extracted as a pure testable module. Advisory locking prevents concurrent write corruption. render-todos.js is called outside the lock (separate write). .planning/todos.jsonl path is hardcoded — projects with different layouts need adjustment.

name: statusline tool workflow
description: Go binary that renders a 16-widget, 3-line ANSI statusline for Claude Code. Reads Claude Code's statusline JSON from stdin, fetches and caches GitHub PR/CI status and weather data, and outputs formatted terminal display. Built via build.sh, installed to ~/.claude/statusline/.
trigger: Every Claude Code turn (settings.json statusLine command runs bash .claude/statusline-command.sh); rebuild via bash tools/statusline/build.sh
notes: Binary is gitignored; config.local.toml (with API key) is gitignored. Only source + config.toml tracked. config.toml has Nashville/America/Chicago hardcoded — sanitize for SoNash. Cache dir deliberately isolated per repo (cache-jason-os/) to prevent cross-repo contamination. Two-file config merge: shared config.toml + machine-local config.local.toml. Platform note: Windows requires chcp.com UTF-8 call at startup.

name: CI security pipeline process
description: GitHub Actions CI pipeline that runs on every PR/push to main: CodeQL semantic analysis, Semgrep static analysis, SonarCloud quality gate + hotspots, dependency vulnerability review, Scorecard supply-chain scoring, and Dependabot auto-merge for minor/patch updates. Each workflow is independent but all gate merge.
trigger: GitHub PR creation/update or push to main (automatic GitHub Actions); weekly schedule for Scorecard and cleanup
notes: sonarcloud.yml requires SONAR_TOKEN secret + sonar-project.properties per-project config (projectKey, organization). All other workflows use only GITHUB_TOKEN (built-in). All action SHAs are pinned for supply-chain security. Semgrep image pinned by tag (not SHA — acknowledged gap). Qodo GitHub App runs separately from workflows but its output is consumed by /pr-review skill. CLAUDE.md §2 declares the security pipeline as part of the security posture.
